api_key.description:
Create an API access token at {authkeys_url}.

Tailscale API access tokens are valid for 90 days, after which a fresh token has to be entered.

credentials.description: Choose how Home Assistant should authenticate with Tailscale.
credentials.title: Authentication method

oauth.description:
Create an OAuth client at {oauth_url} and grant it the **Devices: Core** read scope.

OAuth clients do not expire, so they do not need to be replaced periodically.

reauth_confirm.description:
Tailscale API access tokens are valid for 90 days. Create a fresh one at {authkeys_url}.

To stop having to replace it, reconfigure this entry to use an OAuth client instead, which does not expire.

reauth_confirm_oauth.description: Re-enter the credentials of your Tailscale OAuth client, managed at {oauth_url}.

reconfigure.description: Switch this entry over to an OAuth client, which does not expire. Create one at {oauth_url} and grant it the **Devices: Core** read scope.

user.description:
This integration monitors your Tailscale network, it **DOES NOT** make your Home Assistant accessible via Tailscale VPN.

A Tailnet is the name of your Tailscale network. You can find it in the top left corner in the Tailscale Admin Panel (beside the Tailscale logo).
